title: Oil falls below $70 due to the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz and tensions with Iran
summary: Crude oil plummets to pre-conflict levels as the White House seeks funds for a possible war and maritime insurers anticipate massive losses.

title: Micron's record results spark a global rally in memory chip manufacturers
summary: The US manufacturer's earnings boost shares of Samsung, SK Hynix, and Kioxia, though volatility in South Korea raises doubts about the sustainability of the boom.

title: European defense industry sees a day of contrasts with Rheinmetall, KNDS, and a major order
summary: Rheinmetall plummets due to the cancellation of a frigate project, while another company receives a mega-contract and KNDS announces its IPO in Frankfurt and Paris.

title: The renewables and commodities sector faces a volatile environment due to El Niño and geopolitics
summary: China's largest IPO in four years from a renewable energy group and Iberdrola's batteries in the US arrive amid uncertainty over the climate phenomenon and trade tensions.

title: Gold falls below $4,000 for the first time in seven months
summary: The strengthening dollar and expectations of interest rate hikes pressure the precious metal, which breaks a key support level.

title: Euro hits its lowest level in a year against the dollar
summary: The single currency weakens due to the fall in oil and expectations of a more restrictive monetary policy by the Federal Reserve.

title: Fed warns of up to $700 billion in losses in its stress tests of US banks
summary: Despite the massive potential losses in a global recession scenario, the Federal Reserve considers that the US financial system remains solid.

title: The artificial intelligence sector, between fierce competition and investment opportunities
summary: Anthropic accuses Alibaba of stealing its model, Google loses key talent, and the market debates the value of AI companies as a firm falls in the stock market.

title: Global legal battles in the financial and payments sector
summary: British and German regulators investigate payment systems, a hedge fund sues the CFTC in the US, and Uber faces a legal battle over drivers' rights.

title: Spanish banking restructures: BlackRock, BBVA, Santander, and the neobank debate
summary: BlackRock increases its stake in Telefónica, BBVA reduces capital, Santander negotiates early retirements, and the sector debates the traditional banks' lack of interest in neobanks.

title: Binance leaves Greece in search of MiCA license in another EU country
summary: The cryptocurrency exchange withdraws its application as a provider in Greece and confirms its exit to begin the regulatory process in another EU member state.

title: Wall Street closes mixed with oil weighing on the market
summary: New York indices end the session without a clear direction, with the Dow Jones up and the Nasdaq down, in a day marked by the fall in crude oil.

title: Wendy's shares surge more than 10% in a single day
summary: The fast-food chain's stock experiences a sudden rise without a clear corporate catalyst, vastly outperforming the broader market.

title: Tensions rise between China and Japan over smuggling and territorial disputes
summary: China detains two Japanese citizens for alleged rare earth smuggling, while China's Ministry of State Security warns Japan and the Philippines about border negotiations.

title: European stock markets fall dragged down by defense and technology sectors
summary: The IBEX 35 loses 19,400 points pending tech stocks, while the German DAX closes in negative territory although a defense company manages to save the day with a large order.

title: European automotive sector seeks alternatives and VW sells its marine engines unit
summary: European car manufacturers face difficult choices for their future, while Bain Capital acquires a majority stake in Volkswagen's marine engines unit for 7.4 billion euros.

title: SpaceX issues bonds with oversubscription and faces new challenges
summary: Elon Musk's company places bonds with demand four times higher than supply, while doubts arise about the future of the commercial space industry after recent setbacks.

title: Strong magnitude 6.9 earthquake shakes Aomori prefecture in Japan
summary: The high-intensity quake disrupts Shinkansen bullet train service and mobilizes the Japanese government, which activates the crisis room in the prime minister's office with no tsunami risk.

title: BMO raises Take-Two's price target on possible $80 price for GTA VI
summary: The anticipated release of Rockstar's next video game boosts the company's revenue forecasts, according to BMO investment bank analysis.

title: Fortescue becomes latest Australian miner to face class action lawsuit over sexual harassment
summary: The iron ore giant is sued collectively for alleged sexual harassment, joining a series of similar cases shaking the mining industry in Australia.
